homepage.title: Introduction | Kevin Bonavia MP
homepage.h1: I'm proud to call Stevenage my home, living off the High Street in Old Town. Growing up, my mum and dad instilled in me that if you see a problem, your roll up your sleeves, and you fix it. ​ Ever since, I tried to give back in the best way that I could. I became a solicitor and went on to represent victims of the phone-hacking scandal. I became a councillor, serving as an armed forces champion. ​ In every step of my life, public service ran through my DNA. ​ But I kept encountering problems that a lawyer or a councillor simply couldn't solve. So I stood to be the MP for the town I love - Stevenage, and its vibrant villages of Knebworth, Codicote, Datchworth and Aston. Since then, I've never looked back.
homepage.meta_description:
Kevin Bonavia MP
Member of Parliament for Stevenage, Knebworth, Codicote, Datchworth and Aston
headings: h3: Making a strong start as your MP
h3: A strong presence at home and in Parliament
homepage.text_excerpt: --> Introduction | Kevin Bonavia MP top of page Introduction Personal Promises Online Newsletter Stay in touch Upcoming Events Campaigns Latest News Parliament Access help all year round More Use tab to navigate through the menu items. Kevin Bonavia MP Member of Parliament for Stevenage, Knebworth, Codicote, Datchworth and Aston At the General Election in July 2024, I was elected as the MP for the town I'm proud to call home. After 14 years of failure, I promised to put our community first again. ​ ​ ​ Since then I've been working hard to return the role to its foun ding purpose - public service. This website can be used to find out more about what I'm doing and how I can help you. Personal Promises Get in touch with me Newsletter Sign Up My background I'm proud to call Stevenage my home, living off the High Street in Old Town. Growing up, my mum and dad instilled in me that if you see a problem, your roll up your sleeves, and you fix it. ​ Ever since, I tried to give back in the best way that I could. I became a solicitor and went on to represent victims of the phone-hacking scandal. I became a councillor, serving as an armed forces champion. ​ In every step of my life, public service ran through my DNA. ​ But I kept encountering problems that a lawyer or a councillor simply couldn't solve. So I stood to be the MP for the town I love - Stevenage, and its vibrant villages of Knebworth, Codicote, Datchworth and Aston. Since then, I've never looked back. Becoming your MP Following the General Election, it became the honour of my lifetime to represent our constituency. Whether you voted for Labour, for another party or didn't vote at all, my message to you is clear. I was elected on a platform of returning the role of MP to its founding purpose - public service. I take this responsibility seriously. I made a number of personal promises to you and I'm proud to report that I've made significant progress on some of them - but there is much more work to do. I also stand ready to s crutinise our new government in delivering the change that our country needs after inheriting economic ruin and broken public services. Making a strong start as your MP It's up to me to prove myself to you. After being elected, I tried to make the strongest start. My office inherited a serious casework backlog from the last MP. I've since held 35 advice surgeries, supporting over 100 residents with their local problems. ​ I've hired a full-time team to support me in my role, responded to thousands of policy enquiries, attended hundreds of events in the constituency and started to hold public meetings in our villages. ​ I've started a school trips to Parliament scheme, a programme of personal visits to every school, shop and business in the constituency and launched a cost of living toolkit to support residents this winter. I've spoken up for our area in Parliament 59 times, voted 231 times, consulted local residents on their views towards changes and lobbied ministers to support our town and villages. My Upcoming Events My work in Parliament My Latest News A strong presence at home and in Parliament Sign up to my monthly online newsletter I made a promise to maintain regular contact with residents. You can now sign up to get monthly updates in your inbox about what I’m doing as your MP. This will include my advice surgery dates, information about local events, my stance on important issues and updates on my work in Parliament. Join the already 1000 plus residents who are receiving updates from me today by clicking the button and signing up! SIGN UP HERE Call my office Email my office Write to my office 020 7219 0784 kevin.bonavia.mp@parliament.uk Ground floor right, Osprey house, Primett Road, Stevenage, SG1 3EE © 2024 Promoted By Tom Plater on behalf of Kevin Bonavia MP both at Ground floor right Osprey house, Primett Road, Stevenage, SG1 3EE My Privacy Policy My Acceptable Behaviour Policy bottom of page

pages.title: Campaigns | Kevin Bonavia MP
pages.text_excerpt: --> Campaigns | Kevin Bonavia MP top of page Introduction Personal Promises Online Newsletter Stay in touch Upcoming Events Campaigns Latest News Parliament Access help all year round More Use tab to navigate through the menu items. Campaigns This page is to share news and information on the campaigns and initiatives I am involved with. As our MP, I work with lots of local groups to deliver for Stevenage and our villages. Check here for updates and details on ways to get involved. Pride in Place​ The St Nicholas neighbourhood has been selected to receive £20 million of Government investment over the next ten years through the Pride in Place programme. This is a once-in-a-generation opportunity to restore pride in our area, improve local services and create new opportunities for residents, especially our young people. ​ What makes this programme truly special is that it will be community-led. This is not a scheme imposed from the Council or the Government. Instead, local residents like you will have real power to decide how this funding is spent and what priorities we focus on as a neighbourhood. ​ Over the coming months and beyond, I’ll be working closely with residents, businesses, Stevenage Borough Council and community groups to get things moving. What makes this special is that local people decide exactly what to focus on, and how the funds are spent. Your voice will be heard. ​ I’ll be sharing updates along the way, and by completing the survey you can stay in touch throughout to hear the exciting progress we’re making, together. This is your opportunity to help shape the future of the St Nicholas neighbourhood, now and for the next generation. I hope you’ll join me on this incredible project. ​ I am encouraging every household in St Nick’s to take a few minutes to complete our online survey and share your views on what matters most to you and your family. Have your say and stay in touch by taking the quick survey: Pride in Place survey © 2024 Promoted By Tom Plater on behalf of Kevin Bonavia MP both at Ground floor right Osprey house, Primett Road, Stevenage, SG1 3EE My Privacy Policy My Acceptable Behaviour Policy bottom of page
